architecture.summary: FLAP is a constrained decoding algorithm that guides LLM plan generation by maintaining dynamic dependency graphs for API and workflow steps, then scoring candidate token sequences via a lookahead heuristic that evaluates: (1) semantic alignment of generated thoughts to permitted workflow steps, (2) alignment of generated APIs to permitted APIs based on input-output parameter dependencies, (3) alignment of generated thoughts to user intent, and (4) coherence between generated thoughts and APIs. The system operates on top of any autoregressive LLM without fine-tuning, using a sentence transformer for semantic similarity scoring and enforcing structural constraints during beam search decoding.

biblio.title: FLAP: Flow-Adhering Planning with Constrained Decoding in LLMs

claims.statement: All LLMs fail heavily in zero-shot planning regardless of the setting, especially LLMs fail to preserve the API and flow step dependencies in plans
The average percentage of inconsistent APIs in zero-shot prompting baselines ranges from 60% to 90%
FLAP reduces inconsistent APIs from 90% to 10% for Mpt-7b-instruct in all-flows-in-context setting
FLAP reduces inconsistent APIs from 80% to 15% for Mistral-7b-instruct in all-flows-in-context setting
FLAP on Mistral-7b-instruct outperforms larger LLMs (30B-40B parameter range) in flow step and API error reduction
Beam Search and Nucleus Sampling do not improve overall performance compared to Greedy Search and result in fewer parsable plans
LLMs pretrained on tools (ToolAlpaca) fail heavily in API consistency and result in high API hallucination
FLAP enables quick adaptation to predefined flows without requiring fine-tuning on domain-specific plan or dependency data
